perturbation.original_text: The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue perpetually. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.changed_text: The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years, with automatic renewal for subsequent five (5) year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least six (6) months prior to the end of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by changing the initial term from 'perpetually' to a five-year term with renewal options. This contradicts the immediately following statement indicating the agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor ceases to serve. This introduces ambiguity about whether the agreement is truly perpetual or subject to a fixed term and specific termination conditions. The in-text contradiction happens immediately within the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue for an initial term of five (5) years, with automatic renewal for subsequent five (5) year terms unless either party provides written notice of termination at least six (6) months prior to the end of the then-current term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the term of the license from "perpetually" to "an initial term of five (5) years, with automatic renewal... unless... termination..." This directly contradicts the subsequent statement: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee." The change introduces a finite term where previously it was perpetual, creating a contradiction as to when and how the agreement can terminate. The contradicted text and changed text are located in the same section, causing an immediate contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, the Licensee agrees to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of its business and the operation thereof equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of the Licensor's and the Licensee's businesses as of the date of this Agreement. The Licensee further agrees to use the Licensed Mark in accordance with such quality standards as may be reasonably established by the Licensor and communicated to the Licensee from time to time in writing, or as may be agreed to by the Licensor and the Licensee from time to time in writing. The Licensee agrees to allow the Licensor to conduct reasonable inspection of the quality of the Licensee's services from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, the Licensee agrees that it is *encouraged* to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of its business and the operation thereof. The Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark in accordance with quality standards reasonably established by the Licensor, in the Licensor's sole discretion, and which may be updated and modified at any time without notice to or consent by the Licensee. The Licensee agrees to allow the Licensor to conduct inspections of the Licensee's services at any time without notice. The Licensee is not binded to maintain the quality of its business equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of the Licensor's and the Licensee's businesses as of the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by altering the enforcement of quality control. The original text states that the licensee agrees to *use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality* of its business. The modified version makes maintaining quality now just *encouraged*, while also stating that the Licensor can change quality standards at any time, without notice. This contradicts the original intent of maintaining standards equal to the date of agreement. It also introduces ambiguity regarding the control of the Licensee's brand and quality of business.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In order to preserve the inherent value of the Licensed Mark, the Licensee agrees that it is *encouraged* to use reasonable efforts to ensure that it maintains the quality of its business and the operation thereof. The Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark in accordance with quality standards reasonably established by the Licensor, in the Licensor's sole discretion, and which may be updated and modified at any time without notice to or consent by the Licensee. The Licensee agrees to allow the Licensor to conduct inspections of the Licensee's services at any time without notice. The Licensee is not binded to maintain the quality of its business equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of the Licensor's and the Licensee's businesses as of the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a strong contradiction. The original text emphasizes the Licensee's agreement to *maintain* quality standards equivalent to those at the agreement's date. The modified text weakens this commitment to merely being *encouraged* to maintain quality, while simultaneously granting the Licensor unilateral power to alter those quality standards without notice or consent. This directly contradicts the original intent and introduces significant ambiguity, making it unclear what quality standards the Licensee is actually bound by and undermining the initial agreement to maintain a specific level of quality. Also, the sentence 'The Licensee is not binded to maintain the quality of its business equal to the standards prevailing in the operation of the Licensor's and the Licensee's businesses as of the date of this Agreement.' directly contradicts the original text which required the Licensee to agree to maintain the quality standards.
